backend.changelog: Pre-Generate close-foreign-docs prompt now runs BEFORE attempting to open the BOM root assembly, so leftover non-BOM documents that were blocking SolidWorks Connector's fresh root open get cleared first. 1.1.18 placed the prompt only in the post-engage recheck branch, which never fired when the root wasn't already loaded -- the root auto-open polled 3 minutes then aborted with 'SolidWorks is still loading...'. Also: the partial-close safety gate no longer falls through to the legacy 'close them manually' abort when even a single library / template doc lacks a 3DX plmId; the closeable subset goes through the modal as before and any leftover unmanaged docs surface via a post-close 'Closed X, Y remain' message. Both fixes applied at both prompt sites for consistency. Workstation Apply Update for CadBridge 1.8.48 still needed to benefit from the auto-close half plus the new STEP+3DPDF foreign-docs fix.

cadBridge.changelog: Fixes STEP and 3D PDF being silently skipped when the user has foreign documents open in SolidWorks at Generate time. Three surgical changes: (1) the orchestrator now passes the BOM root's resolved doc handle to the 3D PDF post-loop instead of relying on whatever doc was active when Generate started (which was typically NOT the BOM root when foreign docs were open). (2) The 3D PDF post-loop force-closes any open SW document that isn't a Generate candidate and isn't dirty -- defense in depth in case the BOM root resolution missed; dirty documents are skipped and logged by title rather than auto-closed. (3) VerifyActive now tries ActivateDoc3 (newer SW API) with full title then without extension, plus the legacy ActivateDoc2 as a third fallback; window-swap sleep bumped from 300 to 600 ms because Connector mode is measurably slower than Connected. Workstation Apply Update needed.
